etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, "wood") + phonetic 付(付) (fù) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: 柎(fū, "calyx") combines 木(木) (mù, "wood"), which highlights the botanical context related to plants and flowers, with 付(付) (fù), which provides the phonetic component. The structure suggests a part of a plant, specifically the calyx, which is wooden or firm like 木(木) (mù, "wood").
